title: Childrens Table and Chairs
summary: This cute little table and chairs is the perfect place for your child, grandchild, niece or nephew to play.
description: This cute little table and chairs is the perfect place for your child, grandchild, niece or nephew to play. They can write with chalk on the blackboard top, play with their toys, color, draw or write you a thank you note. Easy to build, uses 1/4 sheet of plywood, 1 1X8X96″ board and 1 1/2 96″ studs.

materials.name: Rust-Oleum Black Chalk Board - Latex Paint

steps.title: Introduction
steps.body: Please be sure to read through complete instructions before you begin any work on this project. Also keep in mind that plywood may be slightly thinner than its stated thickness. 3/4" plywood may actually be 23/32".

steps.title: Cut plywood pieces
steps.body: Cut the table top and chair seats from the 3/4" plywood sheet. Please note that in the picture of the completed chairs the seats were made from edge glued pine. You can do that. However, I believe that the plywood seats, with pine edging, will hold up better. Sand with 100 grit then 150 grit sandpaper.

steps.title: Cut table and chair legs
steps.body: Use your table saw to rip 1 1/4" pine boards from 2X4's. You should be able to get two from each 2X4. Leaving your table saw set at 1 1/4" rip the boards so that they are 1 1/4" by 1 1/4". Then cut the boards to the lengths shown in the attached cut diagram. Sand with 100 grit then 150 grit sandpaper.

steps.title: Cut 3/4" pine pieces pieces
steps.body: Using the dimensions in the following diagram cut the pieces required for assembly.

steps.title: Drill pocket holes in skirt pieces
steps.body: Using a Kreg pocket jig (set to 3/4" for all holes in this design) drill holes, as shown in the attached diagram, in the table and chair skirt pieces, 12 in total. It is a good idea to mark where the holes will be to prevent mistakes. Sand with 100 grit then 150 grit sandpaper.

steps.title: Drill Pocket holes in back rests
steps.body: Using a Kreg pocket jig drill two holes in ends of all four chair back rests. It is a good idea to mark where the holes will be to prevent mistakes. Sand with 100 grit then 150 grit sandpaper.

steps.title: Table leg assembly
steps.body: Using Kreg 1 1/4" coarse pocket screws and Titebond I wood glue attach the table skirts to the legs.They should be flush with the outside of the legs.

steps.title: Paint the table top
steps.body: Apply Rust-Oleum Black Chalk Board paint to the top of the table. Follow manufacturers recommendations.

steps.title: Frame table top
steps.body: Fitting the top frame will require you to cut 45 degree angles on ends of the four table top framing pieces from step 5. Two should be 30" at the outside of the angles and the other two should be 24" at the outside of the angles. You might have to nibble a little off at a time as you fit them to the top. Assemble frame to top using wood glue and 1 1/4" brad nails. As the 3/4" pine edging might be slightly thicker than the plywood top, make sure that the frame is flush with the painted top surface. Any excess should be on the bottom side of the top.

steps.title: Attach table top
steps.body: Using Kreg 1 1/4" coarse pocket screws attach the table leg and skirt assembly to the top.The top should overlap about 1 1/2" on all sides.

steps.title: Chair leg assembly
steps.body: Using Kreg 1 1/4" coarse pocket screws and Titebond I wood glue attach the chair skirts to the legs.They should be flush with the outside top of the front legs. The skirts should attach to the outside edge of back legs 11 1/4" up from the bottoms. See attached diagram. Do this for both chairs.

steps.title: Chair back rest assembly
steps.body: Using Kreg 1 1/4" coarse pocket screws and Titebond I wood glue attach the chair backs to the back legs.They should be 1/4"from the outside of the legs. The top back rest is flush with the top of the legs and the lower back rest is 4" down from the top. See attached diagram. Do this for both chairs. Now use the Kreg Pine Wood Plugs to fill the 16 pocket holes in the back supports. Using 80 - 150 grit sandpaper smooth the plugs flush with the back rest boards.

steps.title: Cut seat tops to final dimensions
steps.body: Mark the two 1" X 1" sections that need to be cut from the two rear corners of the seat tops. Use a jig saw or band saw to make the cuts.

steps.title: Attach the seat edging
steps.body: Use wood glue and 1 1/4" brad nails to attach the seat edging. Attach the back edging first, it should be flush with the top and ends of the seat. Attach the sides next, they should also be flush with the top and both ends of the seat. Finally attach the front edges. They should be flush with the top of the seats and the ends of the side edging. See attached diagram.

steps.title: Attach seat tops
steps.body: Use Kreg 1 1/4" coarse pocket screws to attach the seat tops to the chair skirts. Make sure that the two cut corners of the seat slide completely between the back legs.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Now that the build is complete use 150 - 220 sandpaper to do a once over on all surfaces in preparation for the finish. Clean well to remove all dust. (I use compressed air and clean dry rags) I did not use a stain, just finished with Minwax Polycrylic Satin finish. DO NOT use finish on the Chalk Board area of the table top. I used blue painters tape to mask off that area and then gave the table and chairs three coats of Satin finish following manufacturers instructions.
